unit.name: Training RNN language models with teacher forcing and BPTT
unit.description: Shows how to define the RNN language-model objective, how training is staged over segments, and how gradients are propagated through repeated weights.
unit.summary: Training starts by computing a next-word distribution at each time step and using cross-entropy, equivalently the negative log-likelihood of the actual next word, then averaging that loss per token across a segment [ts=3992s] [ts=4016s] [ts=4058s]. The lecture then explains teacher forcing: during training the model is not allowed to free-run, but is repeatedly fed the ground-truth next token so that each prediction is conditioned on real text prefixes [ts=4142s] [ts=4191s] [ts=4252s]. For optimization, long corpora are cut into manageable segments or fixed-length chunks for batched GPU training, and gradients through repeated recurrent weights are summed across time; in practice truncated backpropagation through time can stop the backward pass after a smaller number of steps [ts=4355s] [ts=4419s] [ts=4501s] [ts=4614s].

key_points.text: The RNN language-model objective is average per-token cross-entropy, i.e. negative log-likelihood of the actual next word.
Teacher forcing feeds the correct next token back into the model during training instead of using the model's own sampled output.
Training corpora are cut into manageable segments, and in practice fixed-length chunks are convenient for vectorized batching on GPUs.
For a repeated recurrent weight matrix, the total gradient is the sum of the gradients from each time step where it appears.
Truncated backpropagation through time keeps the full forward context but shortens the backward pass to speed training.

unit_summary_for_generation: Training starts by computing a next-word distribution at each time step and using cross-entropy, equivalently the negative log-likelihood of the actual next word, then averaging that loss per token across a segment [ts=3992s] [ts=4016s] [ts=4058s]. The lecture then explains teacher forcing: during training the model is not allowed to free-run, but is repeatedly fed the ground-truth next token so that each prediction is conditioned on real text prefixes [ts=4142s] [ts=4191s] [ts=4252s]. For optimization, long corpora are cut into manageable segments or fixed-length chunks for batched GPU training, and gradients through repeated recurrent weights are summed across time; in practice truncated backpropagation through time can stop the backward pass after a smaller number of steps [ts=4355s] [ts=4419s] [ts=4501s] [ts=4614s].

kp_catalog.name: Teacher forcing and backpropagation through time
kp_catalog.description: The standard training setup for autoregressive RNNs, including per-token loss, repeated-weight gradients, and truncated BPTT.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because teacher forcing and backpropagation through time clarifies the standard training setup for autoregressive RNNs, including per-token loss, repeated-weight gradients, and truncated BPTT, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Recurrent neural networks
kp_catalog.description: Shared-parameter sequence models that update a hidden state over time to summarize prior context. Updating a hidden state from current input and previous hidden state at every time step.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because recurrent neural networks formalizes how shared-parameter sequence models that update a hidden state over time to summarize prior context, which later units use as core machinery rather than background context.
